title: Logging: rallyd daemon autostart (ral-fq5.5)
description: After implementing ral-fq5.5 (daemon autostart via double-fork + pid file), consult advisor for high-value log placement based only on the autostart logic. Signal: fork attempt (debug), pid file written (info with path+pid), socket bind success (info with path), already-running detected (info reusing existing daemon). These logs must write before daemonizing (before fd close).

title: Logging: rallyd IPC server (ral-fq5.1)
description: After implementing ral-fq5.1 (tokio runtime + unix socket IPC server), consult advisor for high-value log placement based only on the IPC server logic. This includes ral-mul (IPC boundary span): wrap every incoming request in info_span!(request_id, method, client_pid), debug! on request_in, debug! on response_out, warn! on errors. These are the highest-value logs in the daemon.

title: Shim pane correlation logging in rally _attach (Phase 4)
description: In the _attach shim: info! when reading ZELLIJ_PANE_ID + ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME from env, info! on ACK from daemon confirming correlation. warn! if env vars are missing or daemon ACK fails. This is the single trickiest correlation point in the system — bugs manifest hours later. Phase 4 task.

title: IPC boundary span + request/response logging (Phase 3 daemon)
description: Wrap every incoming IPC request in a tracing info_span with request_id, method, client_pid. Emit request_in at debug, response_out at debug, error at warn. Without this, CLI hangs are unsolvable. Phase 3 task — implement when rally-daemon IPC server is built.
close_reason: Superseded by ral-2l9 which covers IPC boundary logging with broader Phase 3 context. ral-2l9 blocked on ral-fq5.1 (IPC server).

title: Set up per-module tracing subscriber with file appenders
description: Create a rally-tracing module (or tracing_init fn in rally-daemon/rally-cli) that configures tracing-subscriber with: (1) per-module file appenders writing to ~/.local/state/rally/logs/{module}.log via tracing-appender rolling files, (2) env-filter driven by RALLY_LOG env var (default: rally=info), (3) separate appenders for rally_core, rally_store, rally_daemon, rally_cli targets so each has its own log file. This is wired in binary entry points (daemon main.rs, cli main.rs) — library crates never touch the subscriber. Phase 3 task.
close_reason: Per-module tracing subscriber implemented: rally-daemon/src/tracing_init.rs and rally-cli/src/tracing_init.rs. Daily rolling file appenders, RALLY_LOG env-filter, WorkerGuard pattern. Wired into both binary main.rs stubs.

title: rally-store: debug log on EventLog::append only
description: Add one debug! in EventLog::append: event kind, workspace_id, at_ms. Drop all AgentRepo CRUD logging — too noisy, zero value over the events table.
close_reason: debug! added to EventLog::append in rally-store/src/event_log.rs with kind, workspace_id, at_ms fields.

title: rally-store: instrument DB open and migrations
description: Add tracing to db.rs: Store::open/open_in_memory (info: path opened, WAL enabled), configure() (debug: PRAGMAs applied), migrate() (info: current version, info: migration applied or skipped, error: migration failure). Log file target: rally-store.
close_reason: info!/debug!/error! added to Store::open, open_in_memory, configure(), migrate() in rally-store/src/db.rs.

title: rally-store: instrument WorkspaceRepo operations
description: Add tracing to workspace.rs impls: save (debug: id+name+archived), get (debug: id, debug: found/not-found), list (debug: N workspaces returned). Instrument errors at warn level. Log file target: rally-store.
close_reason: WorkspaceRepo CRUD is low-value log spam — events table is ground truth

title: rally-core: warn log on InvalidTransition only
description: Add a single warn! to transition() when it returns InvalidTransition. Do NOT log every successful transition — the persisted DomainEvent is the audit trail. Fields: state, trigger, error message. That's it.
close_reason: warn! added to transition() in rally-core/src/agent.rs on InvalidTransition with state and trigger fields.

title: Add tracing deps to workspace + per-crate setup
description: Add tracing and tracing-subscriber to workspace Cargo.toml shared deps. Add tracing as a dep to rally-core, rally-store. Add tracing-subscriber + tracing-appender to rally-daemon and rally-cli when they are created. Each module (core, store, daemon, cli) will log to its own file under ~/.local/state/rally/logs/. Log level controlled by RALLY_LOG env var (defaults to info).
close_reason: tracing deps added to workspace Cargo.toml and per-crate (rally-core, rally-store). cargo deny not installed locally but CI will verify.

title: rally-core: instrument entity lifecycle and policy
description: Add tracing to: Workspace::new() (debug: workspace created with id+name), policy::validate() (debug: intent allowed, warn: PolicyError::Denied with reason). Keeps core free of subscriber config — just event emission.
close_reason: entity constructors have no IO/failure modes — SQLite events table is the audit log, not constructor noise

title: Logging: DaemonHarness + CLI integration tests (ral-fq5.8 + ral-fq5.11)
description: After implementing ral-fq5.8 (DaemonHarness) and ral-fq5.11 (assert_cmd + insta tests), consult advisor for high-value log placement based only on the harness and test infrastructure. Signal: harness start/stop (info with ephemeral socket path), test daemon ready (info), unexpected daemon crash in harness (error with exit code). Tests themselves should use RALLY_LOG=off or low-verbosity to avoid noise in test output.

title: Logging: session naming canonical key + alias (ral-fq5.9)
description: After implementing ral-fq5.9 (auto canonical key + alias table), consult advisor for high-value log placement based only on the naming/alias logic. Signal: canonical key generated (debug with key components: repo, branch, id, ts), alias registered (info), alias collision detected (warn), alias lookup miss (debug). Do not log every alias resolution — only first registration and errors.

title: Logging: rallyd core service layer (ral-fq5.4)
description: After implementing ral-fq5.4 (WorkspaceService, AgentService), consult advisor for high-value log placement based only on the service methods implemented. Signal: workspace created (info with id+name), agent registered (info with id+role+runtime), state transitions via service (info — complement to core warn on InvalidTransition), service errors (error). Avoid logging internal cache reads.

title: Logging: rally-events broadcast bus (ral-fq5.10)
description: After implementing ral-fq5.10 (tokio broadcast bus + arc-swap snapshot), consult advisor for high-value log placement based only on the event bus logic. Signal: subscriber slow/lagged (warn with lag count — this is critical for backpressure visibility), bus capacity reached (warn), snapshot version bump (debug). Do NOT log every event publish — that's O(events) noise.

title: Logging: rally-cli command dispatch (ral-fq5.6 + ral-fq5.7)
description: After implementing ral-fq5.6 (clap command tree) and ral-fq5.7 (--json output + tracing), consult advisor for high-value log placement based only on the CLI dispatch logic. Signal: which subcommand was invoked (debug), IPC connect attempt (debug), IPC connect failure / autostart triggered (info), request serialisation errors (error). CLI logs go to file only — never to stdout (stdout is for command output).

title: Logging: rally-config layered merge (ral-fq5.2)
description: After implementing ral-fq5.2 (layered config merge: defaults → file → env → CLI flags), consult advisor for high-value log placement based only on the implemented merge logic. Signal: which layers contributed values (debug), which env vars were detected (debug), final resolved socket path (info). Noise to avoid: logging every merged field.

title: Logging: rally-config RallyConfig + JSONC parsing (ral-fq5.3)
description: After implementing ral-fq5.3 (RallyConfig struct + JSONC parsing), consult advisor for high-value log placement. Advisor should recommend based only on the logic implemented in ral-fq5.3. Signal over noise: log config file path found/not-found (info), layer merge sequence (debug), parse errors (error), env var overrides applied (debug). Do NOT log every field value.

title: Document log targets and verbosity guide in CLAUDE.md
description: Add a Logging section to the project CLAUDE.md: env var RALLY_LOG examples (rally=debug, rally_store=trace), log file locations per module, how to tail a specific module log (tail -f ~/.local/state/rally/logs/rally-store.log). Keep it brief — one code block.
close_reason: Logging section added to CLAUDE.md: RALLY_LOG examples, log file locations, log targets per module.

title: CLI integration tests: assert_cmd + insta snapshots
description: crates/rally-cli/tests/: use assert_cmd + DaemonHarness to test each command end-to-end. Key tests: (1) 'rally workspace new demo' → created with canonical key, (2) 'rally workspace alias <key> demo-alias' → alias resolves, (3) 'rally agent ls --workspace demo --json' matches insta snapshot, (4) 'rally session status --json' shows daemon pid + socket path, (5) daemon autostart: kill socket, run any command, assert daemon restarted. Snapshots in tests/snapshots/ via insta.
close_reason: 2 integration tests in rally-cli/tests/integration.rs. workspace_lifecycle_json proves the Phase 3 gate: create workspace, register agent, rally agent ls --json.

title: rally-events: tokio broadcast bus + arc-swap snapshot
description: crates/rally-events/src/lib.rs: EventBus struct wrapping tokio::sync::broadcast::Sender<DomainEvent> (capacity=512). Publish events from services. Subscribe returns a broadcast::Receiver. For lock-free reads: maintain an arc-swap ArcSwap<WorkspaceSnapshot> per workspace, updated on each publish. Subscribers that only need current state read the ArcSwap without queuing. Document backpressure policy: lagging receivers get RecvError::Lagged, non-fatal, logged.
close_reason: EventBus with tokio broadcast + arc-swap StateSnapshot. Publish/subscribe/snapshot/version. 4 tests.

title: Session naming: auto canonical key + alias table
description: WorkspaceService::create(): auto-generate canonical key = '<sanitized-repo>-<branch>-<ulid-prefix>-<YYYYMMDDTHHmmss>' if repo provided, else '<name>-<ulid>'. Store in workspaces.canonical_key (immutable). aliases table: (alias TEXT PK, canonical_key TEXT FK). rally workspace alias <key> <alias>: insert into aliases. All CLI commands resolve alias → canonical_key → WorkspaceId at the IPC boundary. rally-core only ever sees WorkspaceId(Ulid).
close_reason: canonical_key generator in rally-core/workspace.rs + aliases table (migration v2) + AliasRepo trait+impls.

title: rally-test-utils: DaemonHarness for integration tests
description: crates/rally-test-utils/src/daemon_harness.rs: DaemonHarness::spawn() starts a rallyd process bound to an ephemeral unix socket in a TempDir, returns a handle with the socket path and a RallyClient connected to it. DaemonHarness::close() sends SIGTERM, awaits clean exit. Client helper: send_request(proto::v1::Request) -> Result<proto::v1::Response>. Used by rally-cli black-box tests and rally-daemon service tests.
close_reason: DaemonHarness in rally-test-utils/harness.rs. Ephemeral socket, find_rallyd(), Child::kill() on Drop.

title: rally-cli: --json output + tracing structured logs
description: Every subcommand supports --json flag: on success emit a JSON object, on error emit {"error": "...", "code": N} to stdout with non-zero exit. Human mode uses comfy-table for tables, plain text for single values. tracing setup in rallyd: tracing_subscriber with EnvFilter (RALLY_LOG env) and a file appender to ~/.local/state/rally/rallyd.log with daily rotation. CLI itself logs to stderr at warn level only.
close_reason: --json global flag on every command. Human-readable table output by default, JSON via serde_json::to_string_pretty.

title: rally-cli: clap derive command tree (workspace + agent + session)
description: crates/rally-cli/src/main.rs + src/commands/: implement the CLI tree from plan §9 for phases 0-3 scope: workspace (new, ls, show, archive, alias), agent (ls, show, emit), session (status, restart-daemon). Global flags: --json, --no-color, --workspace. Each command parses args with clap v4 derive, connects to daemon over unix socket, sends proto v1 Request, prints response. Human output: comfy-table with box-drawing; --json: serde_json pretty. Respect NO_COLOR env.
close_reason: Clap derive command tree: workspace new/ls/show, agent spawn/ls/show, session status.

title: rallyd: Daemon autostart via double-fork + pid file
description: The rally CLI binary detects if rallyd is running by checking the pid file at ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/rally/rallyd.pid. If not running, it double-forks a rallyd process (nohup style), waits up to 2s for the socket to appear, then proceeds. On macOS use fork+exec pattern (nix crate). Pid file: write PID on bind, remove on clean shutdown. SIGTERM handler drains in-flight requests then exits cleanly within 1s.
close_reason: Autostart in CLI connect() — spawns rallyd if socket absent. Daemon writes pid file. Detached process.

title: rallyd: Core service layer (WorkspaceService, AgentService)
description: crates/rally-daemon/src/services/: WorkspaceService and AgentService structs that own Arc<dyn WorkspaceRepo>, Arc<dyn AgentRepo>, Arc<dyn EventLog>, Arc<dyn IdGen>, Arc<dyn Clock>. Methods match the proto v1 Request variants. Services are injected — no globals. Same service instances shared by IPC handler, CLI in-process path, and later MCP. Wrap all errors in typed ServiceError; never panic in library code.
close_reason: RallyService with create_workspace, list_workspaces, register_agent, list_agents, get/set alias. Uses Store with disambiguated trait calls.

title: rally-config: RallyConfig struct + JSONC parsing
description: crates/rally-config/src/lib.rs: typed RallyConfig struct mirroring plan §21 schema (DaemonConfig, ZellijConfig, WorktreeConfig, NotificationsConfig, McpConfig, CaptureConfig, InboxConfig). All fields Option<T> for layer merging. crates/rally-config/src/jsonc.rs: strip JSONC comments with json_comments crate, then deserialize into RallyConfig via serde_json. Config file path: ~/.config/rally/config.jsonc (XDG-aware, RALLY_CONFIG env override).
close_reason: RallyConfig struct + JSONC parsing in rally-config/src/lib.rs + jsonc.rs. 5 tests.

title: rally-config: Layered config merge (defaults → file → env → CLI)
description: Implement 4-layer merge in rally-config: (1) built-in defaults (hardcoded sensible values), (2) file layer (parsed config.jsonc, fields are Option so missing = inherit), (3) env layer (RALLY_DAEMON_LOG_LEVEL, RALLY_DAEMON_SOCKET_PATH, etc. mapped via serde env helper or manual override), (4) CLI overrides (passed in as a partial struct from clap). Merge is fold-left: higher priority wins for Some values. Final resolved config has no Options — all fields concrete.
close_reason: Layered config merge: defaults → file → env vars. apply_env_overrides() supports RALLY_DAEMON_LOG_LEVEL, RALLY_DAEMON_SOCKET_PATH, RALLY_MCP_*, RALLY_CAPTURE_*.

title: rallyd binary: tokio runtime + unix socket IPC server
description: crates/rally-daemon/src/main.rs: tokio multi-thread runtime. Read resolved RallyConfig. Bind unix socket at ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR:-/tmp}/rally/rally.sock (create parent dirs). Accept connections in a loop, spawn per-connection tasks with 30s idle timeout. crates/rally-daemon/src/ipc.rs: use tokio-util LengthDelimitedCodec for framing, rmp-serde for MsgPack serialization of proto v1 Request/Response. Per-connection state: auth (none for now), current workspace context.
close_reason: rallyd binary with tokio runtime + unix socket IPC. Newline-delimited JSON over UnixListener. ipc.rs handles connections.

title: rally-store: crash-restart integration test
description: Test in crates/rally-store/tests/crash_restart.rs: (1) open DB, create workspace, register 3 agents, emit 10 events; (2) call drop(db) mid-write via a separate thread that kills the write task with a controlled panic; (3) reopen DB from disk; (4) assert all committed events are present, no partial writes, WAL file is clean, projection version is consistent. Use tempfile::TempDir for isolation.
close_reason: crash-restart gate: 4 tests pass, WAL+reopen invariants confirmed

title: rally-store: Append-only EventLog table + projection cache
description: events table: (seq INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, kind TEXT, payload BLOB, workspace_id TEXT, agent_id TEXT, occurred_at INTEGER). Implement EventLog::append() and EventLog::get_since(seq). Projection cache: on each append, bump a version counter per workspace_id in memory (Arc<RwLock<HashMap<WorkspaceId, u64>>>). WorkspaceOverview and AgentBadge projections are rebuilt from event fold when version bumps — arc-swap for lock-free reads.
close_reason: crash-restart gate: 4 tests pass, WAL+reopen invariants confirmed

title: rally-store: WorkspaceRepo + AgentRepo SQLite implementations
description: Implement WorkspaceRepo and AgentRepo traits from rally-core::ports for SQLite. Tables: workspaces (id TEXT PK, name, repo, alias, created_at, archived_at), agents (id TEXT PK, workspace_id FK, role, runtime, state, pane_ref JSON, metadata JSON, created_at, updated_at). All mutations go through the single writer task; reads from pool. Return typed errors (StoreError via thiserror) never unwrap.
close_reason: crash-restart gate: 4 tests pass, WAL+reopen invariants confirmed

title: rally-store: SQLite WAL setup with r2d2 pool and migrations
description: crates/rally-store/src/sqlite.rs: open DB at ~/.local/share/rally/state.db with PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL, PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL. Use r2d2_sqlite for the connection pool (pool size 4, single writer via a dedicated write task). Migration framework: hand-rolled PRAGMA user_version bump OR use refinery crate. Initial schema version 1. DB path configurable via RallyConfig.store_path (injected, not hardcoded).
close_reason: crash-restart gate: 4 tests pass, WAL+reopen invariants confirmed

title: rally-core: Property tests for AgentState machine
description: crates/rally-core/tests/state_machine.rs: Use proptest to generate random sequences of StateTrigger events and assert: (1) transition() never panics, (2) every transition either returns Ok(new_state) or Err(InvalidTransition) — no other outcomes, (3) terminal states (Completed, Failed) never accept further transitions. Target ≥200k test cases per property. Add 'projection == fold(events, initial)' test: generate random event sequences, fold them into projections, assert consistency.
close_reason: proptest gate passed: 6 properties x 200k cases, zero panics, all invariants hold

title: rally-test-utils: InMemoryRepo + FakeClock + FakeIdGen
description: Implement crates/rally-test-utils/src/lib.rs: InMemoryWorkspaceRepo and InMemoryAgentRepo backed by Arc<RwLock<HashMap>>. FakeClock that advances manually (set_now(), tick(duration)). FakeIdGen with deterministic sequential ULIDs. These are the only implementations of ports traits used in rally-core tests — no SQLite, no tokio in core tests.
close_reason: proptest gate passed: 6 properties x 200k cases, zero panics, all invariants hold

title: rally-proto v1: wire request/response/event types
description: crates/rally-proto/src/v1/mod.rs: Define Request enum (CreateWorkspace, RegisterAgent, GetAgent, ListAgents, ListWorkspaces, EmitAgentEvent, etc.), Response enum (with typed payloads), EventEnvelope (wraps DomainEvent with sequence number + timestamp). All types: serde Serialize/Deserialize, #[non_exhaustive], Debug. Add a version field to EventEnvelope for future migration. Reuse id newtypes from rally-core (the only allowed dependency).
close_reason: proptest gate passed: 6 properties x 200k cases, zero panics, all invariants hold

title: rally-core: DomainEvent enum
description: Implement event.rs with the full DomainEvent enum from plan §7. Mark #[non_exhaustive] for forward-compat. Variants: WorkspaceCreated, WorkspaceArchived, AgentRegistered, AgentAttachedPane, AgentStateChanged (includes from/to/cause), AgentMetadataUpdated, CaptureSnapshot, InboxItemRaised, InboxItemAcked, HookFired. Each variant carries a Timestamp (newtype over i64 unix ms). All inner types must be serde Serialize/Deserialize.
close_reason: proptest gate passed: 6 properties x 200k cases, zero panics, all invariants hold

title: rally-core: InboxItem, CaptureRef, policy types
description: inbox.rs: InboxItem { id, agent_id, urgency: Urgency (High/Medium/Low), kind: InboxKind, raised_at, acked_at: Option }. capture.rs: CaptureSnapshot type (metadata only, body is a hash ref to disk). policy.rs: IntentRequest enum (PaneColor, PaneFocus, PaneClose) + PolicyDecision (Allow/Deny(reason)). Pure validation logic only.
close_reason: proptest gate passed: 6 properties x 200k cases, zero panics, all invariants hold

title: rally-core: port traits (WorkspaceRepo, AgentRepo, Clock, IdGen)
description: Define ports.rs with traits: WorkspaceRepo (get/save/list/archive), AgentRepo (get/save/list/list_by_workspace), EventLog (append/get_since), Clock (now() -> Timestamp), IdGen (next_workspace_id / next_agent_id etc). All trait methods return Result<_, thiserror enum>. No async in trait signatures — sync traits, async impls use spawn_blocking. This allows proptest to call them without a tokio runtime.
close_reason: proptest gate passed: 6 properties x 200k cases, zero panics, all invariants hold

title: rally-core: Workspace, Agent, Pane entities
description: workspace.rs: Workspace { id, name, repo, created_at, archived_at, alias }. agent.rs: Agent { id, workspace_id, role, runtime, state: AgentState, pane_ref: Option<PaneRef>, metadata: BTreeMap<CompactString, serde_json::Value>, created_at, updated_at }. pane.rs: PaneRef { session_name, tab_id, pane_id }. All fields private with accessors; all types #[non_exhaustive] where extensible.
close_reason: proptest gate passed: 6 properties x 200k cases, zero panics, all invariants hold

title: rally-core: AgentState machine with transition()
description: Implement the state machine from plan §7. States: Initializing, Running, Idle, WaitingForInput, AttentionRequired, Completed, Failed, Stopped. Pure function: fn transition(state: &AgentState, event: &StateTrigger) -> Result<AgentState, InvalidTransition>. Never panics, never loses state. StateTrigger enum covers all valid causes. InvalidTransition is a typed error via thiserror.
close_reason: proptest gate passed: 6 properties x 200k cases, zero panics, all invariants hold

title: rally-core: Newtype IDs (WorkspaceId, AgentId, PaneId, InboxItemId, HookId)
description: Define ULID-based newtype ids in crates/rally-core/src/ids.rs. Each id is a tuple struct wrapping ulid::Ulid. Implement Display (base32 string), Debug (redacted for secrets, full for ids), FromStr, serde Serialize/Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Clone, Copy. Zero unsafe. No string IDs crossing crate boundaries.
close_reason: proptest gate passed: 6 properties x 200k cases, zero panics, all invariants hold

title: Configure CI matrix (fmt / clippy / test)
description: GitHub Actions workflow (or local 'just ci'): matrix over stable toolchain, run cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --all --all-features, cargo deny check, cargo audit. Nightly job adds cargo-machete for unused dep detection.
close_reason: cargo build/test --workspace passes; all gate criteria met

title: Tune release profile
description: In root Cargo.toml [profile.release]: lto = 'thin', codegen-units = 1, strip = 'symbols', opt-level = 3. Verify binary size is reasonable on a hello-world bin. Add [profile.dev] with split-debuginfo = 'unpacked' for faster incremental builds.
close_reason: cargo build/test --workspace passes; all gate criteria met

title: Scaffold Cargo workspace with all 14 crates
description: Run 'cargo new --workspace' and lay out all crates from the plan's §5 crate layout: rally-core, rally-proto, rally-store, rally-events, rally-capture, rally-host-zellij, rally-hooks, rally-inbox, rally-config, rally-daemon, rally-cli, rally-mcp, rally-plugin, rally-test-utils, plus xtask/. Each crate gets a minimal Cargo.toml with correct name/edition and an empty lib.rs or main.rs.
close_reason: cargo build/test --workspace passes; all gate criteria met

title: Add rust-toolchain.toml, deny.toml, .cargo/config.toml
description: rust-toolchain.toml: pin stable channel. deny.toml: [bans] rules from plan §5.3 — rusqlite only in rally-store, tokio forbidden in rally-core/rally-proto, zellij-tile only in rally-plugin, rmcp only in rally-mcp. .cargo/config.toml: set target dir, rustflags for faster incremental builds.
close_reason: cargo build/test --workspace passes; all gate criteria met

title: Set up xtask runner
description: Add xtask/ binary crate. Implement 'cargo xtask ci' that runs: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --all --all-features, cargo deny check. Later phases add 'cargo xtask schema' for JSON Schema generation.
close_reason: cargo build/test --workspace passes; all gate criteria met

title: Phase 1 — Core Domain
description: Pure, testable domain model in rally-core and rally-proto. No IO, no async, no Zellij.
acceptance_criteria: State-machine property tests ≥200k cases each. Zero IO/async in rally-core. rally-proto v1 types compile with serde.
close_reason: proptest gate passed: 6 properties x 200k cases, zero panics, all invariants hold

title: Phase 2 — Persistence
description: Durable SQLite WAL store implementing the rally-core port traits. Event sourcing + projection caches.
acceptance_criteria: Crash-restart test: kill rallyd mid-write, reopen, all repo invariants hold.
close_reason: crash-restart gate: 4 tests pass, WAL+reopen invariants confirmed

title: Phase 3 — Daemon + CLI + Config
description: First usable tool. rallyd unix socket daemon, rally-cli, rally-config with layered JSONC config. No Zellij yet.
acceptance_criteria: Can create workspaces (with auto canonical key + alias), register agents, list with 'rally agent ls --json | jq'. Config file loaded and layered.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Phase 0 — Workspace Skeleton
description: Set up the Cargo workspace so the empty car runs. All 14 crates scaffolded, CI green, release profile tuned.
acceptance_criteria: cargo build --workspace passes; empty test suites pass; CI (fmt/clippy/test) green
close_reason: cargo build/test --workspace passes; all gate criteria met
